---
title: "ICES-DOME Pilot Database"
subtitle: "A pilot study to evaluate the database structure using the ICES-DOME data"
---

This site presents the initial version of the database design and its potential applications using the publicly available [ICES-DOME](https://www.ices.dk/data/data-portals/Pages/default.aspx) dataset.

## Source Data

- Thematic views page at ICES-DOME: [
Contaminants and biological effects of contaminants in biota](https://dome.ices.dk/views/ContaminantsBiota.aspx)

### Search criteria

#### Parameter Group

 * Metals and matalloids (Cobalt, Copper, Manganese, Molybdenum, Selenium, Zinc)
 * Major inorganic constituents (Iodine)

### Count of Sediment Sites
The table below summarizes the number of sediment sites collected across 19 countries between 1985 and 2024.

{{< include _db-setup.qmd >}}

```{ojs}
//| output: false

aq = require("arquero")

// --- Query: long format counts per cruise type, year, and element ---
counts_long = db.query(`
    SELECT country,
           year,
           parameter,
           COUNT(*) AS count
    FROM (SELECT DISTINCT p2.project_id,
                          p2.country,
                          s2.site_id,
                          s2.sample_id,
                          CAST(s2.year AS TEXT) year,
                          s.param,
                          UPPER(SUBSTR(p1.param_description, 1, 1)) || LOWER(SUBSTR(p1.param_description, 2)) AS parameter
          FROM sediment s
                   JOIN parameter p1 ON p1.param = s.param
                   JOIN sample s2 ON s2.sample_id = s.sample_id
                   JOIN site s3 ON s3.site_id = s.site_id
                   JOIN project p2 ON p2.project_id = s.project_id
          WHERE p1.param_description IN ('cobalt', 'copper', 'iodine', 'manganese', 'molybdenum', 'selenium', 'zinc')) t
    GROUP BY country, year, parameter
`)

// --- Pivot to wide format using Arquero ---
elements_wide = ["Cobalt", "Copper", "Iodine", "Manganese", "Molybdenum", "Selenium", "Zinc"]

counts_wide = aq.from(counts_long)
  .groupby("country", "year")
  .pivot("parameter", "count")
  .orderby("country", "year")
  .rename({ country: "Country", year: "Year" })
  .objects()
```

```{ojs}
Inputs.table(counts_wide, {
  columns: ["Country", "Year", ...elements_wide],
  layout: "auto",
  rows: 20
})
```

## Database Design and Creation

After creating the ER (Entity–Relationship) diagram, the tables were implemented in a single SQLite file containing 10 tables. All required data were extracted from the ICES-DOME export file and imported into the corresponding tables.

- See the [DB Schema](./db-schema.qmd) page for details on the database architecture.

## Geospatial Analysis

To facilitate efficient core and sample selection, distances to the nearest coastline were calculated for the ICES-DOME data. In addition, the nearest country and municipality, as well as the sea and ocean names, were identified based on the core locations.

- See the [Distance Calculation](./distance-to-coast.qmd) page for the methods and results of calculating the distance to the nearest coastline.
- See the [Location Names](./location-names.qmd) page for the estimation of location names from geographic coordinates.
- See the [Interactive Map](./distance-interactive-map.qmd) page for an interactive visualization of site locations with meta-information.

## Data Export

Data can be exported for both exploration and submission purposes.

- See the [Export to Tabular File](./data-export.qmd) page for a description of a single tabular file format (`.tsv.gz`) that is extracted and merged from all database tables for data exploration.
- See the [Sediment Particle Size Fractions](./sediment-fractions.qmd) page for a description of the conversion from raw grain-size measurements to standardised sediment particle fractions.

## Tools

- See the [Pilot DB Viewer](./pilot-db-viewer.qmd) page for a read-only view of the pilot database.
- See the [Sediment Trace Element Map](./sediment-map.qmd) page for an interactive map view of the trace elements.
- See the [Code Lookup Browser](./code-lookup-browser.qmd) page for ICES vocabulary codes used in the database.
